title: Acilepis setigera
intro: Acilepis setigera, also known as the Bitter Acilepis, is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Acilepis setigera include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Acilepis setigera is commonly used in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other infections.
taxonomy: Acilepis setigera is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the tribe Gnaphalieae, a group of plants characterized by their small, daisy-like flowers. The genus Acilepis is part of the larger group Gnaphalieae, which includes over 2,000 species of plants from around the world. Acilepis setigera is native to Western Australia, where it grows in a variety of habitats including dry forests and grasslands. The classification of Acilepis setigera is based on its morphology, genetics, and phylogenetic relationships with other species.
